content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom indices visible. Let me focus on atom C:8 (carbon with index 8) and identify its neighbor atoms by examining the bonds connected to it.

From the image, I can clearly see that atom C:8 is connected to:
- C:7 (carbon with index 7) - to the left
- N:9 (nitrogen with index 9) - below/right
- C:19 (carbon with index 19) - above/right
- H:54 (hydrogen with index 54) - below/left

Looking at the bonding pattern, C:8 appears to be a tetrahedral carbon with four covalent bonds. The connections are:
1. Bond to C:7 (carbon)
2. Bond to N:9 (nitrogen) 
3. Bond to C:19 (carbon)
4. Bond to H:54 (hydrogen)

I can confirm these are all direct covalent bonds based on the visual representation where atoms are connected by lines/bonds.

Regarding the geomconf_index, from the file name "geomconf_5597.xyz", I can see that the geomconf_index is 5597.

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom C:8 are:
- Atom index 7, element C
- Atom index 9, element N  
- Atom index 19, element C
- Atom index 54, element H

And the geomconf_index is 5597.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 8, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
